assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Ambiguities and Clarifications Needed' section explicitly flags 5 ambiguities: (1) 'Performance targets undefined' — 'Search should be faster' lacks quantifiable thresholds, (2) 'Relevance criteria unspecified' — 'Results should be more relevant' doesn't define relevance, (3) 'Filter scope undefined' — 'Some kind of filtering capability' doesn't specify which filters, (4) 'Non-functional requirements lack thresholds' — 'Should be fast enough' is not testable, (5) 'Backward compatibility scope unclear'. Additionally, each task description includes an 'Ambiguity flag' paragraph calling out specific ambiguities.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 17: '"Better UI" excluded from scope: The "Better UI" requirement is marked as non-MVP and cannot be planned without design mockups or a frontend repository in the project's Repository Registry. This requirement is excluded from the current plan.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 task files contain the required template sections: ## Repository, ## Target Branch, ## Description, ## Files to Modify (or ## Files to Create), ## Implementation Notes, ## Acceptance Criteria, ## Test Requirements, ## Verification Commands, ## Dependencies. Optional sections (API Changes, Reuse Candidates) are included where applicable and omitted where not. This matches the template format from task-description-template.md.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: Each task labels assumptions explicitly: task-1 line 10: '**Ambiguity flag:** ...This task assumes (Assumption A2, A5)...' and line 12: '**Assumption:** Performance improvement targets p95 search latency under 500ms (Assumption A1)...must be confirmed before implementation.' task-2 line 10: '**Ambiguity flag:** ...This task assumes (Assumption A2)...must be confirmed with the product owner.' task-3 line 10: '**Ambiguity flag:** ...This task assumes (Assumption A3)...must be confirmed with the product owner before implementation.' task-4 line 10: '**Ambiguity flag:** ...This task assumes (Assumption A1)...must be confirmed with the product owner.' The impact-map.md also has a dedicated 'Assumptions (Pending Clarification)' section with A1-A5.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend' as a single repository name: task-1 line 1-2, task-2 line 1-2, task-3 line 1-2, task-4 line 1-2.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain all required sections: task-1 has ## Target Branch (line 4), ## Description (line 7), ## Acceptance Criteria (line 34), ## Test Requirements (line 40). task-2 has ## Target Branch (line 4), ## Description (line 7), ## Acceptance Criteria (line 40), ## Test Requirements (line 47). task-3 has ## Target Branch (line 4), ## Description (line 7), ## Acceptance Criteria (line 41), ## Test Requirements (line 49). task-4 has ## Target Branch (line 4), ## Description (line 7), ## Acceptance Criteria (line 29), ## Test Requirements (line 37).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs (line 7), modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (line 91), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (line 93), common/src/db/query.rs (line 27), tests/api/search.rs (line 113). Files to Create: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the established pattern of migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs (line 19) with a new migration number. No invented paths found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: task-1 Implementation Notes reference: 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs for module structure', 'common/src/db/query.rs for existing query patterns'. task-2 references: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs for service method structure', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. task-3 references: 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs'. task-4 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs'. All tasks reference specific file paths and code patterns (SeaORM, Axum Query extractors, PaginatedResults, AppError, assert_eq pattern).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 lines 4-5, task-2 lines 4-5, task-3 lines 4-5, task-4 lines 4-5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain properly formatted digest comments: task-1 line 54: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:526625b271abfc4135fd7f0c08a66156e1051e7f74cef83828268e22ce021db2' (64 hex chars). task-2 line 63: 'sha256-md:cd3280ae0a54c29699d7de6c07808ebbf62df025870d9fd070fcd26405974168' (64 hex chars). task-3 line 67: 'sha256-md:e3f23a65f2647ebb1a2298eb749a17c327f704ab2c6d999fe027496e100a1979' (64 hex chars). task-4 line 54: 'sha256-md:a137b83634ac4f052560b075cf287c3d9a88cbf58c2245074e58263b837f4bcc' (64 hex chars). All use the correct '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:' prefix with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention-aware enrichment is present in any task description. The Implementation Notes reference docs/constraints.md sections (e.g., 'Per docs/constraints.md §5.2', 'Per docs/constraints.md §5.4', 'Per docs/constraints.md §5.11') but do not reference CONVENTIONS.md at all. The repo-backend.md fixture lists a CONVENTIONS.md file, and the convention-applicability-rules.md requires that applicable conventions include the prescribed rationale format 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'. No such rationale appears in any task. Convention-aware enrichment was not performed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture (feature-ambiguous.md) has Priority: Normal. The impact-map.md correctly notes 'Priority: Normal (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all tasks)'. However, none of the 4 task description files contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of priority. The tasks only have the standard template sections. The priority inheritance is documented as intent in the impact map but not actually present in the task output files.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0, and CLAUDE.md has no ### Jira Field Defaults section so the scope defaults to 'both'. The impact-map.md correctly identifies this: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all tasks — fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. However, none of the 4 task files contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of fixVersions. The fixVersions inheritance is stated as intent but not actually present in the task outputs.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: There is no separate summary comment file in the outputs directory. The impact-map.md contains a 'Priority and Fix Version Propagation' section at the bottom (lines 51-53) stating 'Priority: Normal (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (inherited from TC-9002, will be propagated to all tasks)'. However, this is in the impact map, not a summary comment posted on the feature issue (Step 6c). No output file represents the summary comment that would be posted to the feature issue, so the assertion cannot be confirmed as satisfied.
